summary: Replaced plain-text T+ notation and date strings in all 16 briefing files with semantic <time datetime='YYYY-MM-DD'> elements. 1425 <time> elements added across 16 files.

date_mapping.reference: T+Xd maps to 2026-05-06 + X calendar days

wrap_targets.dateline_spans: Compilation timestamp token inside <span class='dateline'>. datetime= taken from rt-stamp data-this attribute (UTC). Briefings 001-013 use T+HH:MM:SS.000 format; 014 uses T+2d HH:MM:SS; 015 uses T+~13d real-elapsed. All mapped to absolute ISO 8601.
wrap_targets.date_divs:
T+Xd
timeline entries. Both simple (T+Xd) and range (T+Xd–T+Yd) forms handled — range divs receive two wrapped elements, en-dash separator preserved as plain text.
wrap_targets.ts_day_divs:
entries with day-notation (T+Xd
HH:MM) representing the calendar date of a meeting or exchange. Only the T+Xd token is wrapped; the time and location text following the
is left as-is.
wrap_targets.signoff_meta:
T+Xd inside and

— the briefing compilation date.
wrap_targets.footer_colophon: T+Xd references in the colophon footer ('Compiled by Helm on Sunday, T+18d.' and 'Next: ... T+32d.').
wrap_targets.inline_prose: T+Xd tokens in body copy paragraphs, what-divs, bar-labels, metric-foot divs, and list items.
wrap_targets.index_entry_nums: <div class='entry-num'> T+Xd spans in the briefings index. Includes the featured-byline and entry-num spans for B014 (T+~55h real-elapsed) and B015 (T+~13d real-elapsed) — targeted replacement, both mapped to datetime='2026-05-15'.
wrap_targets.index_entry_stats: T+Xd elements (split T+155d pattern — the wraps both the T+155 prefix and the d suffix together).
wrap_targets.briefings_014_015: Real-elapsed notation. 'T+55h real-elapsed', 'T+55:12:38 real-elapsed', 'T+~55h · 2026-05-15', 'T+~13d real-elapsed', 'T+~13d · 2026-05-26' all wrapped with datetime='2026-05-15' per the timeline mapping. The T+~55h in 014's dd element and T+~13d in 015's dd element are wrapped individually, with 2026-05-15 and 2026-05-26 as separate adjacent <time> elements.

not_wrapped.css_comments: T+~48d in /* SITE-NAV injected by nav_rollout.py · T+~48d */ inside <style> blocks, and T+~33d in the site-nav canonical comment. Not user-facing DOM content.
not_wrapped.ts_duration_divs:
T+HH:MM:SS
session transcript elapsed-time stamps (durations within a meeting, not calendar dates). e.g. T+00:11:24 in briefing 002 transcript, T+00:00:21 through T+40:22:09 in briefing 001 founding session.
not_wrapped.rt_stamp_pill: Explicitly excluded per dispatch. The pill already carries data-t0 and data-this ISO attributes for JS parsing.
not_wrapped.html_tag_attributes: datetime=, style=, href= attribute values inside HTML tags — protected by the inline-prose skip-range logic.
not_wrapped.approximate_prose_markers: Inline references like 'T+~31 hours real-elapsed' and 'T+21h compile' in 014/015 body copy. These are approximate windows without a parseable absolute date — conservative per the dispatch mandate.

ambiguous_cases.ts_day_vs_duration: The <div class='ts'> class is used for both meeting datestamps (T+28d format) and session transcript elapsed markers (T+HH:MM:SS format). The migration distinguishes them by regex: T+\d+d suffix = calendar date (wrapped); T+\d{2}:\d{2} format = duration (skipped). No ambiguity at the individual element level.
ambiguous_cases.forward_horizon_markers: Markers like 'T+200d' and 'T+285d' appear as projected future dates. Wrapped with their computed absolute dates (T+200d = 2026-11-22, T+285d = 2027-02-15). The <time> element makes no claim about whether the date has occurred.
ambiguous_cases.range_spans_in_date_divs: T+99d–T+102d in date divs represents a range of event coverage. Each bound is wrapped independently. The en-dash separator is preserved as plain text between the two <time> elements.
ambiguous_cases.index_b014_b015_folio: The B014/B015 folio entries in index.html use T+~55h and T+~13d real-elapsed notation. These are structural identifiers for the briefings (analogous to T+172d for B013). Mapped to datetime='2026-05-15' and treated as dates, not approximate narrative references.

verification.style_block_clean: All <style> blocks verified clean via Python — no <time> elements injected into CSS
verification.rt_stamp_clean: rt-stamp div untouched in all 16 files
verification.ts_duration_clean: All T+HH:MM:SS ts-class entries remain unwrapped across briefings 001 and 002
verification.ts_day_wrapped: T+Xd entries in ts-class divs wrapped in briefings 003-013
